# UC-14.3.41 · Cellular Connectivity Quality & Signal Strength Monitoring

## Description

Tracks LTE/4G signal strength and network latency to predict connectivity issues and plan network upgrades.

## Value

Tracks LTE/4G signal strength and network latency to predict connectivity issues and plan network upgrades.

## Implementation

Edge Hub cellular module reports RSSI (signal strength -140 to 0 dBm), SINR (signal-to-interference noise ratio dB), network latency (ms), and network type (LTE Band, 4G, etc.) to edge-hub-health index every 5 minutes. Strong signal: RSSI > -80 dBm. Acceptable signal: RSSI -80 to -100 dBm. Poor signal: RSSI < -100 dBm. Track carrier (AT&T, Verizon, etc.) and band for capacity planning. Alert if signal drops below -100 dBm or latency exceeds 500ms (indicates backhaul congestion or dead zone).

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (cellular module), Splunk OT Intelligence.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-health` sourcetype=edge_hub cellular_signal, `index=edge-hub-logs` cellular_connect_event.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Edge Hub cellular module reports RSSI (signal strength -140 to 0 dBm), SINR (signal-to-interference noise ratio dB), network latency (ms), and network type (LTE Band, 4G, etc.) to edge-hub-health index every 5 minutes. Strong signal: RSSI > -80 dBm. Acceptable signal: RSSI -80 to -100 dBm. Poor signal: RSSI < -100 dBm. Track carrier (AT&T, Verizon, etc.) and band for capacity planning. Alert if signal drops below -100 dBm or latency exceeds 500ms (indicates backhaul congestion or dead zone).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edge-hub-health sourcetype=edge_hub
| stats latest(rssi_dbm) as signal_dbm, latest(sinr_db) as sinr, latest(latency_ms) as latency,
        latest(network_type) as net_type by host, cell_id
| eval signal_quality=case(
    signal_dbm > -80 AND sinr > 15, "EXCELLENT",
    signal_dbm > -90 AND sinr > 5, "GOOD",
    signal_dbm > -100, "FAIR",
    1=1, "POOR")
| stats avg(latency) as avg_latency by signal_quality, host
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cellular Connectivity Quality & Signal Strength Monitoring** — Tracks LTE/4G signal strength and network latency to predict connectivity issues and plan network upgrades.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-health` sourcetype=edge_hub cellular_signal, `index=edge-hub-logs` cellular_connect_event. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (cellular module), Splunk OT Intelligence. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edge-hub-health; **sourcetype**: edge_hub. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edge-hub-health, sourcetype=edge_hub. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, cell_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **signal_quality** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by signal_quality, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Signal strength heatmap, latency trend, network type distribution.

## SPL

```spl
index=edge-hub-health sourcetype=edge_hub
| stats latest(rssi_dbm) as signal_dbm, latest(sinr_db) as sinr, latest(latency_ms) as latency,
        latest(network_type) as net_type by host, cell_id
| eval signal_quality=case(
    signal_dbm > -80 AND sinr > 15, "EXCELLENT",
    signal_dbm > -90 AND sinr > 5, "GOOD",
    signal_dbm > -100, "FAIR",
    1=1, "POOR")
| stats avg(latency) as avg_latency by signal_quality, host
```

## Visualization

Signal strength heatmap, latency trend, network type distribution.
